PLAINTIFF DRIP DROP, INC.'S BRIEF IN OPPOSITION TO DEMURRER OF DEFENDANTS SOS HYDRATION, INC., BLANCA LIZAOLA AND JAMES MAYO FILED BY PLAINTIFF DRIP DROP INC., A DELAWARE CORPORATION
CASE MANAGEMENT STATEMENT (TRANSACTION ID # 55032157) FILED BY DEFENDANT MICHAEL PAGE INTERNATIONAL, INC. A DELAWARE CORPORATION JURY DEMANDED, ESTIMATED TIME FOR TRIAL: 10.0 DAYS

LAW AND MOTION 302, RULING: A R G U E D. AT THE CONCLUSION OF ARGUMENT, THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING ON DEFENDANTS SOS HYDRATION INC., BLANCA LIZAOLA AND JAMES MAYO'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT WITH MODIFICATION AS TO CAUSES OF ACTION 12 & 13. THE COURT SUSTAINS THE DEMURRER WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO CAUSES OF ACTION 3, 4, 11, 14, 15, 16, 19, AND 21. THE CALIFORNIA UNIFORM TRADE SECRETS ACT PREEMPTS/DISPLACES THOSE CLAIMS. THOSE CLAIMS ARE BASED ON THE SAME NUCLEUS OF FACTS THAT UNDERPIN THE STATUTORY MISAPPROPRIATION OF TRADE SECRET CAUSE OF ACTION. PLAINTIFF'S REFERENCE TO NON-TRADE-SECRET-PROPRIETARY-CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION DOES NOT SAVE THE CAUSES OF ACTION. CALIFORNIA DOES NOT RECOGNIZE A PROPERTY RIGHT IN "CONFIDENTIAL" OR "PROPRIETARY" INFORMATION THAT DOES NOT MEET THE STATUTORY DEFINITION OF A TRADE SECRET. SEE SILVACO DATA SYS. V. INTEL CORP., (2010) 184 CAL.APP.4TH 210, 239 FN.22 (OVERRULED ON OTHER GROUNDS IN KWIKSET CORP. V. SUPERIOR COURT, (2011) 51 CAL.4TH 310). THE COURT SUSTAINS THE DEMURRER WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO CAUSES OF ACTION 17, 18, AND 20. THOSE STATUTORY CLAIMS DO NOT EXPRESSLY ALLUDE TO TRADE SECRETS IN A PARTICULAR SETTING. SEE SILVACO DATA, 184 CAL.APP.4TH AT 241. THE COURT SUSTAINS THE DEMURRER WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO CAUSES OF ACTION 6, 8, AND 10. CALIFORNIA DOES NOT RECOGNIZE AN AIDING AND ABETTING BREACH OF CONTRACT THEORY OF LIABILITY. PLAINTIFF'S REMEDY IS TO SEEK LEAVE TO PLEAD AN INTERFERENCE WITH CONTRACT OR PROSPECTIVE ADVANTAGE CAUSE OF ACTION. OVERRULED AS TO CAUSES OF ACTION 12 & 13 AS TO MPI. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER CSR# 5976.

Notice Of Motion And Motion By Defendants To Remove Attorneys Eyes Only Designation From Plaintiffs C.C.P. Section 2019.210 Disclosure Of Alleged Trade Secrets
Off Calendar Jun-01-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. RICHARD J. IDELL, TEL. NO. 986-2400 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. NICOLAS SAEIZ OF THE SHARTSIS FRIESE LLP, TEL. NO. 773-7217 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANTS.

RULING - A R G U E D. DEFENDANTS SOS HYDRATION INC.,, BLANCA LIZAOLA, JAMES MAYO, MICHAEL PAGE INTERNATIONAL, INC. Motion To Remove Attorneys Eyes Only Designation From Plaintiffs C.C.P. Section 2019.210 Disclosure Of Alleged Trade Secrets - Movants and Defendants SOS Hydration, Inc., Blanca Lizaola, James Mayo and Michael Page International Inc. (collectively "Defendants") Motion to Remove "Attorneys Eyes Only" Designation from Plaintiff's C.C.P. 2019.210 Disclosure of Alleged Trade Secrets is GRANTED. Having failed to notice the request for monetary sanctions, and having failed to note the request for sanctions on the caption page as required under the Code, Defendants' request for monetary sanctions against Plaintiff Drip Drop, Inc. ("Drip Drop") is DENIED. (See Word-Minutes for details). Order signed in open court. Judge Pro-tem: Douglas Robbins; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Not reported.

Off Calendar Apr-02-2014 Continued To May-28-14 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-04-2014 Sustained
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Richard Munzinger, Esq. of Shartsis Friese, Tel. 415.773.7340, for defendants and moving party;
Ory Sandel, Esq. of Idell & Seitel LLP, Tel. 415.986.2400, for plaintiff and opposing party;
Mark J. Nagle, Esq. of Murphy Rosen LLP, Tel. 310.899.3300, for defendant MPI, by Court Call.

RULING: A R G U E D. At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopted its tentative ruling on DEFENDANTS SOS HYDRATION INC., BLANCA LIZAOLA AND JAMES MAYO'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT with modification as to causes of action 12 & 13. The Court sustains the demurrer without leave to amend as to causes of action 3, 4, 11, 14, 15, 16, 19, and 21. The California Uniform Trade Secrets Act preempts/displaces those claims. Those claims are based on the same nucleus of facts that underpin the statutory misappropriation of trade secret cause of action. Plaintiff's reference to non-trade-secret-proprietary-confidential information does not save the causes of action. California does not recognize a property right in "confidential" or "proprietary" information that does not meet the statutory definition of a trade secret. See Silvaco Data Sys. v. Intel Corp., (2010) 184 Cal.App.4th 210, 239 fn.22 (overruled on other grounds in Kwikset Corp. v. Superior Court, (2011) 51 Cal.4th 310). The Court sustains the demurrer without leave to amend as to causes of action 17, 18, and 20. Those statutory claims do not expressly allude to trade secrets in a particular setting. See Silvaco Data, 184 Cal.App.4th at 241. The Court sustains the demurrer without leave to amend as to causes of action 6, 8, and 10. California does not recognize an aiding and abetting breach of contract theory of liability. Plaintiff's remedy is to seek leave to plead an interference with contract or prospective advantage cause of action. Overruled as to causes of action 12 & 13 as to MPI. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer CSR# 5976.
